mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and guidance, which suppresses equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series decelerate, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising marginal costs, prompting markets to price in imminent monetary tightening and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO accelerates during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent directional flip across business cycle stages.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative or flat 20-day returns when payrolls rise and unemployment falls, or the interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal expanding residential investment and rising household wealth effects, which stimulate durable goods consumption and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 105 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply-side bottlenecks and weak manufacturing demand, causing markets to downgrade near-term earnings forecasts and triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105 during recovery phases, or the threshold effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.05).
